reviews.text: DO NOT USE! I REPEAT DO NOT USE!!! If you can avoid using this firms services please do so. They will  take advantage of you especially if you need help. reviews.text:
I cannot recommend Cody Dowden highly enough.

Before hiring Cody, I was represented by Norwood and Norwood, who had my case for six weeks with no progress. I was repeatedly told they were unable to get responses from the prosecutor and that there were no updates. After weeks of waiting and uncertainty, I ultimately made the decision to terminate their representation.

Hiring Cody was the best decision I could have made.

Cody took over my case and had it resolved in less than a week.

From the very beginning, he was professional, kind, responsive, and extremely efficient. He clearly understood the situation, communicated expectations upfront, and followed through immediately. His assistant, Anne, was also fantastic. She was organized, responsive, and genuinely helpful throughout the entire process.

To add another layer of complexity, I DO NOT live in Arkansas, I live in Utah, so navigating a legal issue in another state was stressful in itself. Cody handled everything seamlessly and made the entire process straightforward and reassuring.

In addition to the incredible results, his fees were very reasonable, especially given how quickly and effectively he resolved the matter. The contrast between my previous experience and working with Cody was night and day.

After going through the frustration of firing one firm and trusting a new attorney, I can honestly say Cody far exceeded my expectations. He did exactly what he said he would do and delivered results when it mattered most.

If you are in Arkansas and need legal representation, I wholeheartedly recommend Cody Dowden. Five stars isn’t enough. I would give him 10 stars without hesitation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Chelsea, your review is greatly appreciated. It's easy for clients to be lured into believing certain firms are better because of slick advertising or fancy offices. The reality is, when you work for yourself your name and your reputation are the key to your success. I take pride in the fact that my reputation is of an attorney who gets things done and works hard for his clients. When your name is on the line, you make it happen. When you trust a firm whose name is not the one who is handling your case, you take a gamble.
I'm glad I was able to make things happen so quickly for you. I wish you all good things going forward.

reviews.ownerResponse:
I am so glad you left this review! You are absolutely correct: while I strive not to be a jerk (this is a client-based business, after all), I deal in facts, not emotions. My job is to tell people what they need to hear, not what they want to hear. If a client wants an attorney who is never going to lie to them, sugar coat things, or be a doomsayer, but just relay the reality of their situation and what we can do to improve it, then I'm their man. There are plenty of attorneys who will tell clients what they want to hear to get their money, and they don't figure it out until the end of their case with an outcome they were told wouldn't happen. That's not how I operate, and my clients are better off for it.
Good luck to you, and merry Christmas.

A Life-Saving Attorney – The Best Decision I Ever Made

I can say without a doubt that hiring Chris Birch of Birch Law Firm was one of the best—and most important—decisions of my life.

I was facing a completely false charge that could have led to five years of incarceration. The situation was terrifying and felt hopeless at times, but from the moment Chris took on my case, everything changed. He listened to me, believed in my innocence, and fought for me with unmatched dedication and skill.

Chris didn’t just represent me—he defended my future, my freedom, and my life. He dissected every detail, built a rock-solid defense, and exposed the truth in a way that left no room for doubt. Thanks to his tireless work and expertise, I was cleared of all charges and able to walk away with my life intact.

This man is not just an attorney—he’s a fighter, an advocate, and in my case, a true lifesaver. If you’re facing a serious legal battle and need someone in your corner who will not give up on you, I cannot recommend Chris Birch highly enough. He is everything you could hope for in a defense attorney and more.

Thank you, Chris. I owe you everything.

I am writing to formally express my deep disappointment and concern regarding the quality of legal representation I received from you. I entrusted you with an important matter, expecting a reasonable level of preparation, professionalism, and advocacy. Unfortunately, that expectation was repeatedly unmet.

I went out of my way to send you relevant materials and documents to aid in my defense—information that could have significantly influenced the outcome had it been properly reviewed and applied. Yet, at each court appearance, it became clear that you had either not reviewed the information or chose to disregard it altogether.

Rather than advocating on my behalf, your approach was to shift responsibility to the prosecution, as if your role was passive rather than proactive. At no point did I feel you were invested in my best interest or the outcome of my case.

I am sharing this not out of hostility, but because clients deserve legal counsel who will show up prepared, fight when needed, and treat each case with the seriousness it deserves. Regrettably, my experience with you reflected none of these qualities.

It is deeply concerning that Kate's decision seemed to be influenced by conversations with the opposing party's attorney. This indicates a lack of integrity and independence on her part, which are essential qualities for any lawyer. A lawyer's primary duty should be to advocate for their client's best interests, not to bow to pressure from the other side.

Overall, my experience with this law firm, particularly with attorney Kate Hill, has left me questioning their professionalism and commitment to their clients.
